response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep web, full-stack, and D-language experience, but he does not meet the role's core mandatory requirements: his only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database experience is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background; his RIDE Capital role was fintech-adjacent but built on Node and TypeScript.
strengths: Broad senior engineering experience across web, backend, desktop, and embedded systems with strong testing and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Some financial-services adjacency at RIDE Capital plus Java exposure and DevOps/CI tooling experience
concerns: Only about seven months of professional C++ experience, ending in 2015 — far below the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or genuine capital-markets/investment-banking domain expertise, and no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and CI/CD fundamentals
